headline: Management Styles
description: Management styles vary by context and culture. Autocratic (top-down decisions), Democratic (participatory), Laissez-faire (hands-off), Transformational (inspire vision), Servant Leadership (leader serves team). Modern approaches favor autonomy, purpose, mastery (Pink). Micromanagement is destructive. Good management adapts style to situation.

citation.name: Drive: The Surprising Truth About What Motivates Us (Daniel Pink)
The Culture Map: Breaking Through the Invisible Boundaries (2025 Updated)
